checks.detail: instruction.md, task.toml, environment/Dockerfile, and tests/test.sh all present.
solution/solve.sh, oracle.yaml, and job.yaml all present.
allow_internet=false, agent.timeout_sec=7200, verifier.timeout_sec=3600, build_timeout_sec=600, agent.user='agent' all defined.
git and tmux are installed via apt-get in the Dockerfile.
tests/ contains extra files beyond test.sh: compute_reward.py and run_all.py.
reasons: compute_reward.py and run_all.py should live in environment/tests/ and be COPYed into the image instead of sitting in the task-root tests/ folder

checks.detail: Stray ANTI_CHEAT.md at task root (no .gitignore present).
reasons: ANTI_CHEAT.md is not in the always-ignore list and no .gitignore exists to exclude it

notes: Move tests/compute_reward.py and tests/run_all.py into environment/tests/ and COPY them into the Docker image so tests/ contains only test.sh.
Remove ANTI_CHEAT.md from the task root or add a .gitignore that excludes it.

checks.detail: task.toml sets [agent] user = "agent".
Dockerfile creates agent user, locks /tests 700, owns /app and /solution to agent, installs strace.
reasons: present: useradd agent (line 42)
present: chown -R agent:agent /app (line 83)
present: chown agent:agent /solution (line 77)
present: mkdir /tests + chmod 700 /tests (lines 72, 98)
present: strace installed (line 11)
present: git + tmux installed (lines 10, 14)
present: /tests hardened with go-rwx recursively except fixtures subtree (line 99)

checks.detail: test.sh locks /logs/verifier 700 then wipes, runs agent binary as agent under strace with env -i, scores outside strace, always exits 0.
reasons: present: chmod 700 /logs/verifier BEFORE rm -rf (lines 39-40)
present: re-locks /tests chmod 700 after Harbor upload (line 69)
present: pkill agent processes before scoring (lines 78-87)
present: env sanitization (unset LD_PRELOAD, PYTHONPATH, etc., lines 74-75)
present: agent binary + build.rs run via su agent under strace -f with env -i (run_all.py lines 117-123; test.sh lines 222-227)
present: timeout wrappers on strace blocks (lines 224, run_all.py 121)
present: strace tripwires for reward file writes + banned execve (run_all.py lines 61-64, 136-153; test.sh lines 230-236)
present: scoring (compute_reward.py) runs OUTSIDE strace and never imports /app code (test.sh lines 366-367)
present: no set -euo pipefail, always exit 0 (lines 84, 121, 150, 165, 235, 274, 325, 370)
present: fallback reward 0.0 via emit_fail -> compute_reward.py --fail on every error path
present: no chown root:root on /logs

checks.detail: runs_agent_code — verifier builds the agent's Rust crate and executes the produced binary on fixtures.
reasons: build.rs and cargo build --release execute as agent under strace (test.sh lines 222-227)
agent binary executed per test case as agent under strace (run_all.py lines 117-123)
scoring phase only diffs captured .oracle/.actual text files — does not import /app

checks.detail: No external language dependencies (no Cargo.toml, no Python third-party packages); apt and rustup install only.
No Python or Rust dependency manifests; verifier/build scripts use stdlib only.
task.toml does not set docker_image; expected during development but required before final delivery.
reasons: docker_image not set in [environment]

notes: Set [environment].docker_image to a prebuilt registry image before final delivery.

checks.detail: Deliverable, flags, env vars, and output behavior are clearly specified.
Instruction states the 2-hour sandbox budget and per-case 60s timeout explicitly.
Reads as a crisp engineering brief, slightly spec-like but appropriate for the task.
95 lines, under the 100-line cap.
Uses an 'Evaluation' section and 'scores 0 for that case' phrasing that surfaces verifier mechanics.
reasons: Section heading 'Evaluation' on line 50
Phrase 'scores 0 for that case' on line 56-57

checks.detail: Mentions Rust, cargo, allowed crates (libc, nix, clap), and fixture locations.

notes: Consider renaming the 'Evaluation' section to something like 'Output Expectations' and rephrasing the per-case timeout note to avoid the word 'scores'.

checks.detail: No Cargo.toml in workspace triggers emit_fail('not_a_rust_project', 0) yielding score 0.0.
Multi-layer hardening: env -i, agent user, strace tripwires for both build.rs and run phase, encrypted oracle with key only in root-only test.sh, /tests at 700, atomic reward write.
Both cargo build (build.rs) and the agent binary execute via `su agent -c` with env -i.
strace -f wraps build.rs and each per-case agent run; logs scanned for reward-file openat and banned execve before scoring.
compute_reward.py uses python3 -ISs, never imports /app, runs from /tests, writes reward atomically via os.replace.
Every failure path calls emit_fail then exit 0; strace/build/run all wrapped with timeout commands.
Each case requires byte-exact diff vs GNU ls output (post trailing-whitespace normalization); any mismatch, timeout, crash, or empty output scores 0 for that case.
Scoring is purely output-equivalence to GNU ls; any Rust implementation that produces matching bytes within 60s scores full credit regardless of algorithm.

Five flag-mode cases (no-flags, -1, -l, -S, -C) sit in one tier; each is independent binary pass/fail so gaming one cannot inflate others, and no case is unreasonably hard within 60s for buffered, syscall-aware Rust code.

Trivial outputs (empty, constants, copying input) cannot byte-match GNU ls over a 10k-entry fixture, and direct shortcuts (calling ls/dir/vdir, shipping the oracle binary) are blocked by execve tripwire, sha256 identity check, banned-crate grep, and build-from-source enforcement.
compute_reward.py runs standalone outside strace under `python3 -ISs`, reads only a verifier-written subscores file, and never imports or touches /app or agent code.
Both success (emit_score) and failure (emit_fail) paths atomically write reward.json with score, subscores list, and additional_data plus a single-float reward.txt; failure path uses subscores=[].

Verifier requires a Cargo project (Cargo.toml + cargo build --release) and picks the binary from target/release/<bin-name>, but instruction.md only says 'implement in Rust' and 'final binary at /app/workspace/rls' without explicitly stating a Cargo crate is required.
reasons: test.sh fails with 'not_a_rust_project' if /app/workspace/Cargo.toml is missing, but instruction.md never names Cargo.toml as a deliverable
Verifier ignores the agent's /app/workspace/rls binary and instead rebuilds from source and selects target/release/<binname>; the agent is told the binary path is the deliverable
Both points are inferable from standard Rust practice (cargo is the canonical Rust build tool), so the gap is recoverable but not explicit

checks.detail: GNU ls behavior, the five flag cases, output normalization (trailing whitespace), env vars (LC_ALL/COLUMNS/TZ/TERM), and the 60s per-case budget are all documented in instruction.md.

Rust toolchain is installed system-wide and on PATH for the agent user, and /app/fixtures/perf_10k plus /app/tests/ exist as promised; however the scoped build never produces examples/basic/ or examples/expected/*.txt, so the workspace examples/README.md points to files that do not exist in this variant.
reasons: environment/build_tests.py:create_examples() returns early when the 'basic' fixture is out of scope, leaving only examples/README.md whose instructions reference missing basic/ and expected/*.txt paths
solve.sh does not validate tool access (it only decrypts the baked oracle ls), which is acceptable for this oracle pattern

checks.detail: solution/solve.sh exists, is non-empty (19 lines), writes the HARBOR_ORACLE_FLAG marker, and decrypts the baked oracle ls to /app/workspace/rls.

The five hidden tier9_performance cases (no flags, -1, -l, -S, -C on perf_10k) are exactly the five cases instruction.md enumerates, so the hidden suite is a structural variant of disclosed requirements.

notes: Add one sentence to instruction.md stating the deliverable is a Cargo crate rooted at /app/workspace/ (with Cargo.toml) that produces target/release/<binname>, so the build-from-source requirement is not implicit.

Either fix examples/README.md to describe what the perf-scoped workspace actually contains (perf_10k fixture under /app/fixtures, tier9 JSON specs under /app/tests), or have create_examples() also seed a small example from perf_10k so the README's diff command works out of the box.

checks.detail: Slim base and apt cache cleaned, but gcc/g++/make duplicate build-essential and bc is unused.
reasons: redundant: gcc/g++/make are already pulled in by build-essential
redundant: bc is installed but never invoked (legacy from old shell/bc scoring; current scoring is Python)

checks.detail: solution/ contains only solve.sh; no stray files.
Hardened pattern is correct; a couple of defenses are belt-and-suspenders given user separation.
reasons: useful: chmod 700 + wipe of /logs/verifier first
useful: re-chmod 700 /tests after Harbor's verify-phase upload resets it
useful: oracle ELF validation guards against tampered oracle.enc
useful: build-from-source enforcement deletes target/release/<bin> before cargo build
useful: strace tripwire for reward.{txt,json} writes and banned execve (ls/dir/vdir/oracle)
useful: env -i + run as agent user (matches the documented hardened model)
useful: oracle vs rls sha256 equality check (binary-copy cheat)
useful: static workspace greps for oracle path / key / reward-hacking strings

redundant: unset LD_PRELOAD/LD_LIBRARY_PATH/PYTHONPATH — verifier runs as root from a fresh exec and never sources agent shell profiles, and the agent (non-root) cannot write /etc/ld.so.preload or system profiles anyway

redundant: pkill -u agent + survival check before strace — agent processes that survive are caught by strace's tripwire on reward writes (and the chmod 700 of /logs/verifier already removes their write target); the dedicated pre-kill is mostly defensive

checks.detail: No individual file exceeds 30MB; total repo is under 1MB.
No dead code or stale configs in environment/workspace; only an examples/README.md placeholder.

notes: Drop gcc/g++/make/bc from the apt-get install list in environment/Dockerfile.
Consider removing the LD_*/PYTHON*/BASH_ENV unset block and the pre-strace agent-kill survival check from tests/test.sh — they are redundant under the documented hardened model.

instruction_quality.task_name: Rust ls clone for large directories
instruction_quality.task_summary: Build a Rust binary `rls` that mimics GNU coreutils `ls` for a 10,000-entry directory, supporting -1, -l, -S, and -C flags. Output must be byte-for-byte identical to GNU ls and each case must finish within a 60-second budget.

measures: /tests baked at chmod 700 with go-rwx stripped except fixtures subtree
Decrypted oracle ls lives in root-only WORK_DIR (mktemp -d, 700)
Oracle binary encrypted with AES-256-CBC, key only in test.sh (root-only)
Single Docker layer for oracle creation+encryption so plaintext never persists
ELF validation after decrypt to detect ciphertext tampering
/usr/bin/ls, dir, vdir replaced with error stubs
build.rs strace catches reward-file openat with write flags during build
Per-case run strace catches reward openat AND banned execve (ls/dir/vdir/oracle basename)
env -i strips ORACLE/FIXTURES/TESTS_DEFS_DIR from agent process env
Prebuilt binaries deleted before cargo build (legacy 'rls' name + all Cargo.toml-resolved candidates)
Binary selected by Cargo.toml bin/package name, not glob
SHA256 hash check rejects byte-identical copy of decrypted oracle
Catches references to /oracle/, .test_data, oracle.enc, decryption key, reward.txt/json, /logs/verifier, uutils/coreutils, ls_backup, dir/vdir paths
pkill -u agent (with retry + refusal to score on survival) before scoring phase
Atomic reward write via tmpfile + os.replace + fsync
compute_reward.py runs python3 -ISs outside strace and never imports /app

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent could vendor GNU coreutils ls source into Cargo project and call into it via FFI/build script
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Not a reward hack per se; the deliverable would still be a built-from-source binary producing matching output. Out-of-scope for reward-hacking audit; would require source-similarity detection.
Documented in spec as inherent to single-container model; agent trace tampering does not affect reward score.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: tests/ in repo contains compute_reward.py and run_all.py beyond test.sh
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Spec recommends only test.sh in tests/, but here Dockerfile does not depend on baked tests/ scripts and Harbor's upload merge replaces them with the canonical copies; no reward-hacking impact.

reward_design.formula: score = WEIGHTS['tier9_performance'] * (sum(case_pass) / 5), with WEIGHTS['tier9_performance']=1.0; cheating multiplier 0.0 on tripwire hit; hard-fail paths return 0.0
reward_design.formula_explanation: Single in-scope tier with five equal-weight binary correctness cases under a 60s per-case timeout, reduced to its pass-rate and clamped to [0,1].

components.name: t9_perf_no_flags
components.metric: byte-exact diff vs GNU ls on perf_10k (no flags)

components.name: t9_perf_one_col
components.metric: byte-exact diff vs GNU ls -1 on perf_10k

components.name: t9_perf_long
components.metric: byte-exact diff vs GNU ls -l on perf_10k

components.name: t9_perf_sort_size
components.metric: byte-exact diff vs GNU ls -S on perf_10k

components.name: t9_perf_columns
components.metric: byte-exact diff vs GNU ls -C on perf_10k

dimension_analysis.name: t9_perf_no_flags
dimension_analysis.notes: Sorted name listing of 10k entries; requires correct LC_ALL=C byte-sort and buffered output.

dimension_analysis.name: t9_perf_one_col
dimension_analysis.notes: Same as no-flags for non-TTY default, but explicit -1 path; trivial once correctness is in place.

dimension_analysis.name: t9_perf_long
dimension_analysis.notes: Hardest case: 10k stat() calls plus mode/owner/group/size/time column formatting must match GNU exactly; tight but achievable within 60s.

dimension_analysis.name: t9_perf_sort_size
dimension_analysis.notes: Size-descending sort with name tiebreak; needs stat for sizes but layout is one-per-line.

dimension_analysis.name: t9_perf_columns
dimension_analysis.notes: Column-packing layout must replicate GNU's COLUMNS=80 column-count selection exactly; classic off-by-one risk but well-scoped.

redundant_items.why: build-essential already installs the GNU C/C++ toolchain and make; listing them separately is duplicate
no shell script in tests/ or environment/ invokes bc — the only mention is a comment in compute_reward.py referring to legacy shell/bc scoring that was replaced by Python

redundant_items.item: unset LD_PRELOAD LD_LIBRARY_PATH PYTHONPATH PYTHONHOME BASH_ENV ENV CARGO_HOME ...
redundant_items.why: agent runs as non-root and cannot mutate /etc/ld.so.preload or root shell profiles; verifier launches in a fresh root exec that doesn't inherit the agent's environment, so these unsets are defensive only
strace -f around the agent build/run already blocks until all descendants exit, and chmod 700 of /logs/verifier earlier removes the write target; surviving processes cannot affect the reward path
